ucs.title: [LLM] FIN5 anti-forensic wipe via Sysinternals SDelete after payment-card staging
ucs.description: Hunts Mandiant FIN5's signature post-exfil cleanup: SDelete (sdelete.exe/sdelete64.exe) executed with multi-pass overwrite flags (-p), recursive (-s) or zero-free-space (-z) on hospitality / restaurant / gaming hosts after RawPOS scraping. Public IR reporting (Mandiant GrrCON 2016, FireEye 2015) explicitly calls out FIN5's use of SDelete to destroy track-data dumps and tooling left in staging directories.
ucs.splunk: | tstats `summariesonly` count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as command_lines values(Processes.parent_process_name) as parent_processes values(Processes.process_path) as image_paths from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.process_name IN ("sdelete.exe","sdelete64.exe") OR Processes.original_file_name="sdelete.exe" OR Processes.process_company="Sysinternals*") by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.process_name _time span=1h | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | where match(command_lines, "(?i)\s-(p\s+\d+|z|s|q|accepteula)(\s|$)") AND NOT match(user, "\$$") AND NOT match(user, "(?i)^(system|local service|network service)$") | eval mitre_technique="T1070.004", actor="FIN5 (Mandiant)" | table firstTime lastTime dest user process_name command_lines parent_processes image_paths actor mitre_technique
ucs.kql:
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(30d)
| where AccountName !endswith "$"
| where AccountName !in~ ("system","local service","network service")
// SDelete identification — by name, original-name, or signed-binary metadata (renamed-binary defeat)
| where FileName in~ ("sdelete.exe","sdelete64.exe")
    or ProcessVersionInfoOriginalFileName in~ ("sdelete.exe","sdelete64.exe")
    or (ProcessVersionInfoCompanyName has "Sysinternals" and ProcessVersionInfoProductName has "SDelete")
// FIN5 anti-forensic flag set: passes, recursive, zero-free-space, accepteula auto-run
| where ProcessCommandLine matches regex @"(?i)\s-(p\s+\d+|z|s|q|accepteula)(\s|$)"
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName, FileName, FolderPath,
          ProcessCommandLine, SHA256,
          ParentImage = InitiatingProcessFileName,
          ParentCmd   = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
          ParentAccount = InitiatingProcessAccountName,
          IsRemoteSession = InitiatingProcessLogonId
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(30d)
| where EventID == 4688
| where Account !endswith "$"
| where SubjectUserName !in~ ("SYSTEM","LOCAL SERVICE","NETWORK SERVICE")
| where NewProcessName endswith @"\sdelete.exe"
    or NewProcessName endswith @"\sdelete64.exe"
    or (CommandLine has_any ("sdelete","-accepteula") and CommandLine matches regex @"(?i)\s-(p\s+\d+|z|s)(\s|$)")
| where CommandLine matches regex @"(?i)\s-(p\s+\d+|z|s|q|accepteula)(\s|$)"
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, NewProcessName, CommandLine, ParentProcessName, TokenElevationType
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: FIN5 SDelete Anti-Forensic Wipe
id: 2f3c8f7e-1a4d-4d2b-9a4f-fin5sdelete001
status: experimental
description: Detects Mandiant FIN5's signature use of Sysinternals SDelete with multi-pass / zero-free-space flags to destroy payment-card staging artefacts after RawPOS scraping in restaurant, gaming and hospitality networks.
references:
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0053/
  - https://www.mandiant.com/sites/default/files/2021-09/rpt-fin5.pdf
author: SOC Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/03
tags:
  - attack.defense-evasion
  - attack.t1070.004
  - attack.g0053
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_image:
    - Image|endswith:
        - '\sdelete.exe'
        - '\sdelete64.exe'
    - OriginalFileName:
        - 'sdelete.exe'
        - 'sdelete64.exe'
  selection_flags:
    CommandLine|re: '(?i)\s-(p\s+\d+|z|s|q|accepteula)(\s|$)'
  filter_machine_account:
    User|endswith: '$'
  filter_system:
    User|contains:
      - 'SYSTEM'
      - 'LOCAL SERVICE'
      - 'NETWORK SERVICE'
  condition: selection_image and selection_flags and not 1 of filter_*
falsepositives:
  - Authorised IT-driven disk sanitisation
  - Decommissioning workflows on retired POS terminals
level: high
ucs.rationale: SDelete is publicly documented as FIN5's anti-forensic tool of choice across multiple Mandiant/FireEye engagements. Most legitimate IT use of SDelete is rare and scoped to known sysadmin hosts; the multi-pass `-p` flag in particular is the canonical FIN5 fingerprint (single-overwrite suffices for most legit wipes). Anchoring on Sysinternals signing metadata in addition to filename defeats trivial
_sigma_issues: sigma parse error: Sigma rule identifier must be an UUID

ucs.title: [LLM] FIN5 stolen-VPN-credential logon followed by net.exe / nltest discovery burst
ucs.description: Catches FIN5's hands-on-keyboard pattern after entering hospitality / gaming victim networks via stolen single-factor VPN/Citrix credentials (Mandiant: 'FIN5 used legitimate credentials to access POS environments via remote access'). External-sourced successful logon followed within 4h by ≥5 distinct Windows discovery commands (`net view`, `net group "Domain Admins"`, `net localgroup`, `nltest /dclist`) — FIN5's documented enumeration runbook before deploying RawPOS to in-scope POS endpoints.
ucs.splunk: | tstats `summariesonly` count min(_time) as logon_time from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication where Authentication.action=success Authentication.signature_id IN ("4624") Authentication.authentication_method IN ("NTLM","Kerberos") (Authentication.src_category="external" OR Authentication.src_category="vpn" OR Authentication.app IN ("Citrix","pcAnywhere","AnyConnect")) by Authentication.dest Authentication.user Authentication.src | rename Authentication.* as * | where NOT match(user, "\$$") | join type=inner dest user [| tstats `summariesonly` count values(Processes.process) as discovery_cmds dc(Processes.process) as distinct_cmds min(_time) as first_disc max(_time) as last_disc from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name IN ("net.exe","net1.exe","nltest.exe","quser.exe","qwinsta.exe","net1.exe") (Processes.process="*view*" OR Processes.process="*group*" OR Processes.process="*localgroup*" OR Processes.process="*/domain*" OR Processes.process="*dclist*" OR Processes.process="*accounts*" OR Processes.process="*trust*") by Processes.dest Processes.user | rename Processes.* as * | where distinct_cmds >= 5] | eval correlation_window=last_disc-logon_time | where correlation_window>=0 AND correlation_window<=14400 | eval actor="FIN5 (Mandiant)", mitre_technique="T1133+T1018" | table logon_time first_disc last_disc dest user src distinct_cmds discovery_cmds actor mitre_technique
ucs.kql:
let LookbackDays = 14d;
let CorrelationWindow = 4h;
let _discovery_args = dynamic(["view","group","localgroup","/domain","dclist","accounts","trust","session","users"]);
// Step 1 — external-sourced successful logon (FIN5 stolen-VPN-cred entry)
let ExternalLogons = DeviceLogonEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where ActionType == "LogonSuccess"
    | where LogonType in ("RemoteInteractive","Network","Interactive","NetworkCleartext")
    | where RemoteIPType == "Public"                       // off-corp source
    | where AccountName !endswith "$"
    | where AccountName !in~ ("system","local service","network service")
    | project LogonTime = Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountName, AccountDomain,
              SourceIP = RemoteIP, Protocol, LogonType;
// Step 2 — within 4h, a burst of Windows discovery cmds on the same host/user
let Discovery = DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where FileName in~ ("net.exe","net1.exe","nltest.exe","quser.exe","qwinsta.exe")
    | where ProcessCommandLine has_any (_discovery_args)
    | where AccountName !endswith "$"
    | project DiscoveryTime = Timestamp, DeviceId, AccountName,
              Cmd = ProcessCommandLine, Bin = FileName,
              ParentImage = InitiatingProcessFileName,
              ParentCmd = InitiatingProcessCommandLine;
Discovery
| join kind=inner ExternalLogons on DeviceId, AccountName
| where DiscoveryTime between (LogonTime .. LogonTime + CorrelationWindow)
| summarize DiscoveryCount = count(),
            DistinctBinaries = dcount(Bin),
            DiscoveryCmds = make_set(Cmd, 25),
            ParentImages  = make_set(ParentImage, 10),
            FirstDiscovery = min(DiscoveryTime),
            LastDiscovery  = max(DiscoveryTime),
            DelaySec = datetime_diff('second', min(DiscoveryTime), any(LogonTime))
            by DeviceName, AccountName, AccountDomain, SourceIP, LogonTime, Protocol, LogonType
| where DiscoveryCount >= 5                              // empirical: FIN5 IR avg ~7 cmds in first hour
| order by LogonTime desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
let LookbackDays = 14d;
let CorrelationWindow = 4h;
let _discovery_terms = dynamic(["view","group","localgroup","/domain","dclist","accounts","trust","session"]);
let ExternalLogons = SecurityEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where EventID == 4624
    | where LogonType in (3, 10, 2)                         // Network, RemoteInteractive, Interactive
    | where Account !endswith "$"
    | where IpAddress !startswith "10."
         and IpAddress !startswith "192.168."
         and not(IpAddress matches regex @"^172\.(1[6-9]|2\d|3[01])\.")
         and IpAddress !in ("-","","::1","127.0.0.1")
    | project LogonTime = TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, TargetUserName, IpAddress, AuthenticationPackageName, LogonType;
let Discovery = SecurityEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where EventID == 4688
    | where Account !endswith "$"
    | where NewProcessName endswith @"\net.exe"
         or NewProcessName endswith @"\net1.exe"
         or NewProcessName endswith @"\nltest.exe"
         or NewProcessName endswith @"\quser.exe"
         or NewProcessName endswith @"\qwinsta.exe"
    | where CommandLine has_any (_discovery_terms)
    | project DiscoveryTime = TimeGenerated, Computer, TargetUserName, CommandLine, NewProcessName, ParentProcessName;
Discovery
| join kind=inner ExternalLogons on Computer, TargetUserName
| where DiscoveryTime between (LogonTime .. LogonTime + CorrelationWindow)
| summarize DiscoveryCount = count(),
            DistinctBinaries = dcount(NewProcessName),
            DiscoveryCmds = make_set(CommandLine, 25),
            FirstDiscovery = min(DiscoveryTime),
            LastDiscovery  = max(DiscoveryTime)
            by Computer, TargetUserName, IpAddress, LogonTime, AuthenticationPackageName
| where DiscoveryCount >= 5
| order by LogonTime desc
ucs.rationale: FIN5 is uniquely characterised by ~exclusive reliance on stolen single-factor VPN/Citrix/pcAnywhere credentials for initial access (T1133+T1078) — they don't drop droppers via email or exploit perimeter CVEs. The discovery burst (T1018) using built-in net/nltest binaries directly after entry is documented in Mandiant's 2016 GrrCON FIN5 talk and FireEye's 2015 disclosure. Anchoring on the chain (ex
